hard.next_step: Confidence 10% June 8th - Follow up demo scheduled Thurs 11th. June 4th - Positive, but short discovery call held, early stage exploration, will require an RFP in due course (no compelling event, likely Q4) next step is to arrange another session to capture more info on the project, provide a conceptual overview for the stakeholders then run a live demo, date TBC w/c 8th Jun. SITUATION ASYAD Group runs fragmented procurement across multiple business units — using a government e-tendering platform, SAP Ariba (for RFQs/tenders), and a partially-deployed SAP S/4HANA ERP (in progress). Each BU operates with different systems and methodologies. IMPACT/PAIN No unified spend visibility; data is siloed across BUs; manual/inconsistent processes; limited strategic supplier depth; slow access to contract information; tail spend poorly managed. PROPOSED RESOLUTION/STRATEGY A flexible, integrated S2P platform that stitches all systems together (including SAP S/4HANA and the government e-tendering tool), provide a control-tower view of spend/data, and is highly configurable.
hard.ais_why: ASYAD is actively pursuing digital transformation as part of Oman's logistics and economic diversification strategy. As a large integrated logistics and transportation group, procurement plays a critical role in managing complex supplier ecosystems and operational spend. The organization is likely to benefit from AI-powered procurement capabilities such as autonomous sourcing, intake management, contract lifecycle management, supplier risk monitoring, spend analytics, and procurement automation. Its focus on operational efficiency, data-driven decision-making, and innovation makes ASYAD a strong candidate for Agentic AI-powered Source-to-Pay (S2P) solutions.

north_star_verdict.headline: Healthy early-stage exploration that has gone quiet: Jamila Al Bukhaly ran a strong 4 Jun discovery and Zycus delivered the 11 Jun live demo, but 25 days on no next working session is booked and the scheduling options she owed have not arrived, while the buyer stays deliberately non-committal (no compelling event, Q4 RFP at the earliest, 'we may stick to what we have'). The relationship is warm and single-threaded through Jamila; the immediate momentum on the deeper source-to-contract demo has stalled.
north_star_verdict.math: Created 5 Jun 2026, entered Formal Evaluation 29 Jun 2026 (Qualified since 5 Jun). Last genuine buyer touch 11 Jun 2026 (live demo + inbound email), 25 days ago as of 6 Jul. 268 days to a 31 Mar 2027 close. Amount raised 100k to 300k on 15 Jun and close pulled in from 21 May to 31 Mar on 15 Jun (rep optimism, not buyer-driven). No buyer-facing deliverable executed and no inbound reply since 11 Jun, and the buyer-owed scheduling options from the 4 Jun call remain outstanding: a stalled immediate action, not yet a cold deal. Pipeline forecast is correct for pre-RFP exploration.
evidence: Discovery/intro call held 4 Jun 2026, Jamila Al Bukhaly and Sameh Bartok articulated pain, priorities and decision structure (Avoma, 4 Jun 2026)
Live demonstration session delivered 11 Jun 2026 (SF event 11 Jun; buyer inbound email 'give us 10mins and we will attend', 11 Jun 2026)
Buyer stated an RFP is required, no compelling event, likely Q4 2026 timing (Next_Step, 4 Jun 2026)
Buyer explicitly non-committal on replacing SAP Ariba: 'We don't have a straight answer... it's yet very premature to decide' (Avoma, 4 Jun 2026)
No booked next session and no buyer inbound since the 11 Jun demo (SF activities, last_activity 11 Jun 2026)
Single-thread: Jamila Al Bukhaly is the only Salesforce contact role on the opportunity

metrics.narrative: Sameh Bartok and Jamila Al Bukhaly articulated clear operational pain on 4 Jun 2026 but attached no numbers to it. Sameh described the strategic driver as a 'control tower perspective' where fragmented systems are 'stitched together' for 'proper reporting and analysis and accurate information across all our businesses', and Jamila named slow contract retrieval ('it may take me a day or two to really find a contract which sits under ports') and tail-spend gaps ('a very robust automated tool... that's 100% something that we need to deep dive and build'). No cost of fragmentation, cycle-time baseline, headcount, savings, or ROI figure was ever stated, so there is no quantified value case to justify a 300k spend into an RFP. Building that baseline with Jamila is a ranked move.
sources: Avoma intro call 4 Jun 2026 (Sameh Bartok, Jamila Al Bukhaly)

economic_buyer.narrative: Sameh Bartok, who introduced himself on the 4 Jun 2026 call as 'senior vice president contracts and procurement' and confirmed his email was on the invite, is the budget authority: he set strategic direction ('our main priority is to have integrated systems... to stitch all of this information together') and framed sanctioning as governed ('there always a business case to be developed and the right sanctioned authority permits that'). He attended the 4 Jun call and the 11 Jun demo but has stayed strategic rather than actively sponsoring, and budget today sits with the in-flight SAP S/4HANA ERP programme, not a ring-fenced S2P line ('there is a budget assigned for the current project... we are actually in the process of implementation of an ERP system'). Sameh is inferred as economic buyer from the conversation and is not yet persisted as a Salesforce contact role, so converting his passive attendance into active sponsorship and confirming a dedicated S2P budget is the gap to close.

decision_criteria.narrative: The buyer named their evaluation criteria explicitly on 4 Jun 2026. Jamila Al Bukhaly led with flexibility and self-service configurability: 'we need a system that is flexible enough... if I need a field to be touched, I need my team here, I don't even need to go back to you... at the click of a button.' Sameh Bartok prioritised a single integrated platform / control-tower reporting that stitches SAP S/4HANA, the government e-tendering tool and the reverse-auction app together, plus supplier-management visibility. Data residency is a hard criterion: 'we have the governance in terms of hosting our data in Oman.' SAP S/4HANA integration and avoidance of core-system customisation were also confirmed. These are confirmed, buyer-stated criteria; the formal weighting will firm up in the Q4 RFP.
sources: Avoma intro call 4 Jun 2026 (Jamila Al Bukhaly, Sameh Bartok)

decision_process.narrative: The process was mapped verbatim on 4 Jun 2026. ASYAD runs a 'center led' operating model: strategic, consolidated procurement projects are owned by the central control tower led by Jamila with Sameh overseeing, while business units (ports, free zones, etc.) run tactical operations. Sameh confirmed 'the center will manage the RFP for the source-to-pay solution, make a decision... in concert with your stakeholders across the business units', and that Jamila specifically will own the S2P RFP. Any new system 'will have to be through a proper tender process in line with our governance', so a formal RFP gated by governance and sanctioned budget authority is the path. No RFP has been issued yet; the buyer flagged Q4 2026 as the likely window with no compelling event forcing it earlier.
sources: Avoma intro call 4 Jun 2026 (Sameh Bartok, Jamila Al Bukhaly)

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics have not been discussed: no MSA, SOW, legal review, procurement channel or InfoSec pack has surfaced on any call or field. As a government-linked Omani holding company running a formal tender, the eventual paper process will run through a public/governed tender with authority sign-off, and data residency ('hosting our data in Oman') will pull an InfoSec and hosting-feasibility review forward once a real RFP lands. Because the deal is pre-RFP and 268 days from close, this is a genuine early-stage unknown rather than a stall. Visibility would come from asking Jamila, during the next working session, whether the S2P purchase requires public tender, what authority approves the award, and what data-residency/InfoSec evidence the tender will demand.
sources: Avoma intro call 4 Jun 2026 (Sameh Bartok on Oman data hosting)

identify_pain.narrative: The pain is confirmed and specific, articulated by both buyer principals on 4 Jun 2026. Sameh Bartok owns the strategic pain: fragmented procurement across business units running different systems (a government e-tendering platform, SAP Ariba for RFQs, a partially deployed SAP S/4HANA ERP, plus Oracle and in-house tools in some BUs), with no unified spend visibility or control-tower reporting. Jamila Al Bukhaly owns the operational pain: limited strategic-supplier depth ('we end up going to refloat a tender two or three times to get the most competent supplier'), slow contract retrieval ('a day or two'), and weak tail-spend management, which Sameh called out as '100% something that we need to deep dive and build.' They pushed back on Dan Quinn's generic pain list where it did not fit (they rejected 'inconsistent tendering' and 'limited requisition control'), which makes the pains they did confirm credible. Quantification is the only missing layer.
sources: Avoma intro call 4 Jun 2026 (Sameh Bartok, Jamila Al Bukhaly)

champion.narrative: Jamila Al Bukhaly, Contract & Procurement Shared Services Senior Manager, is a developing champion. What has developed: she originated the engagement after seeing Zycus at the Gartner symposium, briefed and brought her SVP Sameh Bartok onto the first call, articulated the pain and criteria in detail, and explicitly owns the S2P RFP ('the RFP process, yes, it will be done through us'). What is still missing: she is the only Salesforce contact role (single-thread), the deeper source-to-contract demo has produced no booked follow-up in the 25 days since 11 Jun, she has not yet returned the scheduling options she promised on 4 Jun, and there is no evidence she is actively selling Zycus internally against SAP-ecosystem consolidation. She has access and intent but has not yet demonstrated political drive, so she is a coach-plus rather than a proven champion.
sources: Avoma intro call 4 Jun 2026 (Jamila Al Bukhaly)

competition.narrative: The active competitive dynamic is the incumbent-plus-status-quo, not a named rival bake-off. SAP Ariba is the incumbent RFQ/tender tool and the real threat is SAP-ecosystem consolidation: ASYAD is mid-rollout of SAP S/4HANA ('we are actually in the process of implementation of an ERP system... fully ERP'), which creates gravitational pull toward expanding Ariba rather than adopting a best-of-breed platform. Crucially the buyer is non-committal about displacing Ariba: asked directly if the appetite to replace it exists, Sameh answered 'we don't have a straight answer for it... it's yet very premature to decide' and 'we may stick to what we have.' Coupa, GEP Worldwide and iValua are listed in Competitors__c but were never mentioned on any call or activity, so they are dormant field entries, not evaluated rivals. The wedge is that Zycus integrates with SAP better and cheaper than Ariba does (pre-built S/4HANA connectors), charges suppliers nothing (Ariba's supplier fees were flagged as friction), and offers the configurability and agentic automation the buyer explicitly demanded.
sources: Avoma intro call 4 Jun 2026 (Sameh Bartok, Jamila Al Bukhaly)

deal_movement.summary: Two real buyer touches in June (4 Jun discovery, 11 Jun demo) then 25 days of silence. Rep-side field changes (amount up, close pulled in, ownership to an RVP, stage to Formal Evaluation) reflect internal optimism and account realignment, not fresh buyer engagement.

items.change: Intro/discovery call held; buyer confirmed pain, center-led decision process and Jamila as RFP owner
Rep shared the overview deck to Jamila as promised on the call
Live demonstration session delivered; buyer emailed to confirm attendance
Amount raised 100k to 300k and close date pulled in from 21 May 2027 to 31 Mar 2027 (rep-side, by Dan Quinn)
Stage moved Qualified to Formal Evaluation; ownership moved to Mohamad Alhakim (RVP)

critical_signals.text: The live threat is the status quo: ASYAD is mid SAP S/4HANA rollout and stays deliberately non-committal on replacing the incumbent Ariba, saying on 4 Jun they may just keep what they have.
The 11 Jun live demo landed on the back of a strong 4 Jun discovery, but no next working session has been booked in the 25 days since and the buyer has gone quiet.
Sameh Bartok, SVP Contracts & Procurement and the budget authority, is engaged but passive, and only Jamila Al Bukhaly is actively driving the evaluation.
No S2P budget is ring-fenced (it sits inside the ERP programme), there is no compelling event, and the RFP is Q4 at the earliest, so a no-decision is the primary risk.

competitive_position.summary: The single strongest current threat is inertia plus SAP-ecosystem consolidation, not a rival vendor: ASYAD is mid SAP S/4HANA implementation and said on 4 Jun 2026 it is 'very premature to decide' and 'may stick to what we have', so keeping the fragmented status quo and expanding incumbent SAP Ariba is the live alternative. SAP Ariba is the incumbent RFQ/tender tool under soft review for its supplier charges and usability. Coupa, GEP Worldwide and iValua appear only in Competitors__c and have never been named on a call or activity, so they carry dormant threat. No new call evidence this run, so competitor rankings are carried forward.

competitors.name: Status quo / no decision
competitors.quote: We don't have a straight answer for it... it's yet very premature to decide. We may stick to what we have.
competitors.how_we_win: Create a compelling event: co-build a quantified fragmentation-cost and tail-spend baseline with Jamila and tie it to the SAP S/4HANA go-live so 'stitch it together now' beats 'wait'.

competitors.name: SAP Ariba
competitors.quote: We currently have Ariba SAP at this point of time, but we are looking at different... anyway we're just looking at other potential systems.
competitors.source: Avoma intro call 4 Jun 2026 (Sameh Bartok); SF Competitors__c
competitors.how_we_win: Position Zycus as best-of-breed that integrates with SAP better and cheaper than Ariba (pre-built S/4HANA connectors), charges suppliers nothing versus Ariba's supplier fees, and delivers the configurability the buyer demanded.

competitors.name: Coupa
competitors.quote: Listed in Competitors__c but not mentioned on any call or activity.
competitors.how_we_win: Unknown, no evidence of active evaluation or buyer preference; confirm during RFP scoping whether Coupa is genuinely in the field.

competitors.name: GEP Worldwide
competitors.quote: Listed in Competitors__c but not mentioned on any call or activity.
competitors.how_we_win: Unknown, no evidence of active evaluation; treat as an RFP-stage contender to validate, not a confirmed threat.

competitors.name: iValua
competitors.quote: Listed in Competitors__c but not mentioned on any call or activity.
competitors.how_we_win: Unknown, no evidence of active evaluation; validate at RFP shortlist.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Strong fit on configurability, single-platform integration and supplier economics; exposed on Oman data residency, which is a stated governance hard-requirement Zycus has not yet confirmed it can meet.

items.criterion: Highly configurable, self-service system that adapts to changing governance without vendor dependency
items.quote: We need a system that is flexible enough... if I need a field to be touched, I need my team here... at the click of a button.
items.source: Avoma intro call 4 Jun 2026 (Jamila Al Bukhaly)

items.criterion: Single integrated platform / control-tower reporting stitching SAP S/4HANA, e-tendering and legacy tools
items.quote: The main priority is to have integrated systems... to stitch all of this information together and give us proper reporting and analysis across all our businesses.

items.criterion: SAP S/4HANA integration without customising the core system
items.quote: We are an SAP... if at all, it's S/4 HANA.

items.criterion: No supplier charges (versus Ariba's supplier fees)
items.quote: We don't charge suppliers either... Ariba charge vendors to participate... creates some friction, slows down onboarding.
items.source: Avoma intro call 4 Jun 2026 (Dan Quinn, on buyer's Ariba friction)

items.criterion: Data hosting inside Oman per internal governance
items.quote: We have the governance in terms of hosting our data in Oman.
items.source: Avoma intro call 4 Jun 2026 (Sameh Bartok); Zycus offered UAE/KSA hosting only

items.requirement: Run a live demo focused on sourcing, contract management, supplier risk and performance, plus a glimpse of spend analysis
items.quote: What I think would be more beneficial is the sourcing, contract management, supplier risk, and performance management, then probably a little glimpse of the spend analysis.

items.requirement: Confirm whether ASYAD can host its data inside Oman (not only UAE/KSA) per governance
items.quote: How flexible are you... because we have the governance in terms of hosting our data in Oman.

items.deliverable: Share the Zycus overview deck and Hapag-Lloyd case study with Jamila
items.grounding_quote: I'll share this deck with you afterwards, and share the case study with you afterwards.
items.source: Avoma intro call 4 Jun 2026 (Dan Quinn); SF email 'should be in your inbox now' 7 Jun 2026

items.deliverable: Jamila to return two scheduling options and confirm the deeper source-to-contract working session and RFP timeline
items.grounding_quote: Will you come back to me with those options? Yeah. Sure. Sure.
items.source: Avoma intro call 4 Jun 2026 (Jamila Al Bukhaly)

items.quote: That's 100% something that we need to deep dive and build and mature as an organization.
We have the governance in terms of hosting our data in Oman.

items.area: Economic buyer not persisted as a Salesforce contact role
items.quote: Sameh Bartok named only on the Avoma call and via his stated invite email; only Jamila is a contact role.

items.area: RFP / paper process and approval authority
items.quote: It will have to be through a proper tender process in line with our governance.

best_practice_check.summary: The deal is warm but dangerously single-threaded and momentum has stalled; the highest-leverage moves are to widen the relationship, neutralise the SAP-consolidation reflex early, and give Jamila a quantified case she can carry into the Q4 RFP.
flags: Multi-thread beyond Jamila into an SAP/IT programme owner and a business-unit sponsor before the RFP, or a government-committee decision defaults to SAP consolidation.
Neutralise the SAP-ecosystem pull early: put the pre-built S/4HANA connector story and an Oman data-residency answer in front of their SAP programme owner.
Arm Jamila with a quantified fragmentation-cost and tail-spend baseline so a business case exists to justify the S2P spend into the Q4 RFP.

items.name: Jamila Al Bukhaly I MSc I MCIPS I SPM I Etimad
items.title: Contrats & Procurement Shared Services Senior Manager

champion_strength.summary: Jamila Al Bukhaly is a developing champion: she sourced the engagement at Gartner, brought her SVP to the first call, detailed the pain and criteria, and owns the S2P RFP. What is missing is a second thread, an internal case against SAP consolidation, and follow-through, she has not returned the scheduling options promised on 4 Jun and no next session is booked. Access and intent are there; proven political drive is not yet.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: Zycus led the 4 Jun call with the Merlin agentic and guided-intake narrative (autonomous negotiation, Teams-based intake), and the buyer engaged, but their stated priority is integration and control-tower reporting, with automation a secondary interest, so the AI story is well-positioned but not yet the buyer's primary driver.
ai_positioning_strength.score: strong positioning, buyer not yet AI-led

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS_Score is 9/Strong on organisational fit (digital-transformation mandate, complex supplier ecosystem). Calls corroborate genuine automation appetite, Sameh called robust tail-spend automation '100% something that we need', but the buyer frames the primary need as system integration and unified reporting rather than agentic AI, so the field slightly over-rates current buyer-articulated AI demand.

items.detail: No S2P budget is ring-fenced; funds sit inside the in-flight SAP S/4HANA ERP programme, no compelling event, and the buyer says it is 'very premature', so no-decision is the primary risk.
Single-thread through Jamila; the wider center-led committee and business-unit stakeholders who will shape the RFP are unengaged.
Oman data-residency is a stated governance hard-requirement; Zycus offered UAE/KSA hosting only and has not confirmed an Oman option.
SAP S/4HANA rollout creates internal pull toward expanding incumbent Ariba for ecosystem consolidation, which could overrule the procurement team's best-of-breed preference.
RFP not launched, Q4 2026 timing is vague with no compelling event, so the 31 Mar 2027 close is optimistic for a government tender cycle.
items.source: Next_Step__c 4 Jun 2026; CloseDate field history 15 Jun 2026

confidence_signals.summary: Engagement was warm and two-way through 11 Jun but has thinned: no buyer inbound and no booked next session in the 25 days since the demo, and the buyer-owed scheduling options are overdue.
items: Last buyer touch 11 Jun 2026 (demo + inbound email); 25 days quiet as of 6 Jul 2026
Buyer-owed scheduling options from 4 Jun 2026 not returned
Buyer explicitly non-committal: 'very premature to decide', 'we may stick to what we have'

items.action: Re-engage Jamila to lock dates for the deeper source-to-contract working session.
items.trigger: Buyer promised scheduling options on 4 Jun and has been silent since the 11 Jun demo.
items.expected_effect: Restarts stalled momentum and converts warm interest into a booked next milestone.

items.action: Ask Jamila to bring an SAP/IT programme owner and a business-unit sponsor into the next session.
items.trigger: Single-thread through Jamila; center-led committee will decide the RFP.
items.expected_effect: Widens the relationship so a committee decision does not default to SAP consolidation.

items.action: Co-build a quantified fragmentation-cost and tail-spend baseline with Jamila.
items.trigger: Sameh called robust tail-spend automation '100% something we need' but no numbers exist.
items.expected_effect: Creates the business case that justifies the spend and can seed the Q4 RFP.

items.action: Send a tailored SAP S/4HANA integration and Oman data-residency proof pack to the buyer.
items.trigger: Buyer stated an Oman data-hosting governance requirement and is mid SAP S/4HANA rollout.
items.expected_effect: Neutralises the two objections most likely to favour keeping Ariba.

items.action: Offer to help Jamila frame the S2P RFP requirements around configurability, agentic automation and supplier economics.
items.trigger: Buyer confirmed a Q4 RFP owned by Jamila with criteria still forming.
items.expected_effect: Shapes the RFP toward Zycus strengths before competitors are formally engaged.

commentary.win_position: Win 24 is carried by champion strength +0.30 (weight 15); exec access strength +0.30 (weight 15). Offsetting it: momentum 47 vs stage-expected 52.
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 47 (roughly flat): Verdict 'Slowing' — momentum stalling. Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.deal_risk: Risk 14 is driven by Verdict 'Slowing' — momentum stalling. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 8 is anchored on the stage win (24), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 47, and dragged by risk 14. Read is Early Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Early Read: 2 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, stage integrity). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.

cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. We're behind on this one — the reads below say why and what would change it.

blocks.title: Zycus win position
blocks.read: We're well behind — this one is cold.

blocks.read: The threat is the deal stalling into 'do nothing', not a competitor beating us.

blocks.title: What moves it forward
items: Re-engage Jamila to lock dates for the deeper source-to-contract working session. (by 10 Jul)
Ask Jamila to bring an SAP/IT programme owner and a business-unit sponsor into the next session. (by 17 Jul)
Co-build a quantified fragmentation-cost and tail-spend baseline with Jamila. (by 24 Jul)

customer_preference.evidence: Buyer positive and engaged ('I was very much impressed of what I saw', 'sounds like you've come to the right place') with deeper Zycus-specific questions on configurability, SAP integration and hosting, but no decisive lean and explicitly non-committal ('very premature to decide') (Avoma 4 Jun 2026).

business_case.evidence: Qualitative pain clearly articulated (fragmented systems, no unified spend visibility, slow contract retrieval 'a day or two', weak tail-spend management) but no cost, savings, cycle-time or ROI figure quantified (Avoma 4 Jun 2026).

calls_omitted.call: Zycus x Asyad - Live Demonstration Session (11 Jun 2026)
